reasoning: As of May 1, 2025, there are no credible reports of Russia amassing troops or preparing for a new ground invasion of any country other than Ukraine. The US intelligence community, which accurately warned of the Ukraine invasion months in advance, has not issued any warnings regarding an imminent Russian invasion of Moldova, Georgia, the Baltics, or Kazakhstan in 2024 or early 2025. The Russian military is heavily bogged down in Ukraine and lacks the capacity to open a new conventional front. Furthermore, the question explicitly clarifies that an invasion involves "sending its ground military forces into that country without consent," which rules out deployments to Belarus or African nations at the host governments' invitations. Given the very short remaining time window (two months until July 1, 2025) and the lack of any precursors, the probability of this occurring is virtually zero.
